patch.value: Extract linked test runner orchestration into installable pm package
Move subprocess execution, background runs, PM-context sandboxing, assertion gates, and test-runs lifecycle into a pm-linked-test-runner package while keeping linked-test metadata core.

Linked test records stay core; execution and background orchestration are package-owned; sandbox safety and assertion metadata remain available when package installed; CI and temp-project dogfood cover package and compatibility paths.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-05-14: pm search linked-test-runner package extraction found only closed sandbox runner fixes plus parent pm-59gj; net-new extraction child under pm-59gj.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Research correction 2026-05-14: CLI surface audit recommends keeping linked-test safety/orchestration in core for agent PM reliability, while extracting external test adapters/reporters and specialized output packs instead of moving the whole runner out of core.

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Core linked-test safety and orchestration remain stable; package boundary for adapters/reporters is documented; future packages expose optional handlers/contracts without weakening sandbox safety; focused tests cover any extracted adapter surface.
Keep linked-test metadata and safety-critical orchestration in core while moving optional external test adapters, reporters, and specialized result-format packs into installable pm packages.
Define linked test runner package boundary

message: Correct package boundary after CLI research
